mnemonics.title: WEB APP Characteristics
mnemonics.content:
W-Widely accessible
E-Event-driven (AJAX)
B-Browser-based
A-Any platform
P-Programmable (APIs)
P-Presentation/Business/Data layers
S-Stateless (needs sessions)
-Client-Server separated

acronyms.usage: Primary protocol for web client-server communication
Encrypted version of HTTP for secure communication
Technology for asynchronous web communication without page reloads
Architectural style for designing networked applications
Four basic operations in web application databases
Language for managing database operations in web apps
Security vulnerability where malicious scripts are injected into web pages
Attack that forces authenticated users to submit unwanted requests

visualTips.visualization: Draw two circles connected by bidirectional arrows: 'Browser (Client)' on left, 'Web Server' on right, with HTTP requests/responses flowing between them
Show independent request-response pairs as separate boxes: Request1→Response1, Request2→Response2, with no connecting lines between them, showing session/cookie storage as separate database
Draw a small laptop icon connected to a large server icon with arrow showing most processing happens on server side, small arrow from client showing minimal local processing
Vertical: Stack more layers on single server icon. Horizontal: Add more identical server icons side by side, both pointing to same load balancer

analogies.analogy: Desktop apps are like owning a car (self-contained, maintain yourself); web apps are like using a taxi service (someone else manages the vehicle, you just use it)
Each HTTP request is like a stranger walking into a bank - they must present ID and all information each time; there's no memory of previous visits unless records are kept
A thin client is like a ticket counter - it doesn't process the transaction, just takes your request and gives you the result from the main system
Latency is like the delay in getting a response when texting someone far away vs someone nearby - distance matters
